flags.concern: Pinned group sync: step 5 undercounts the stale-sensitive `group.trackId` reads inside `usePinnedShotGroups`. The loop also keys the `nextGroups` replacement/filter by `group.trackId`, so if the executor self-heals the group earlier in the loop but leaves those later comparisons unchanged, the sync pass can stop matching the same group and leave its `clipIds`/cleanup stale.
flags.evidence: Addressed two significant flags: (1) FLAG-001: Step 5 now explicitly separates `storedTrackId` (for identity-matching against `workingPinnedShotGroups` candidates at lines 365, 370-371) from `resolvedTrackId` (for row/data access), and includes the corrected trackId in the nextGroups output. Previously the plan undercounted stale reads in the sync loop and the self-heal could cause the later map/filter to stop matching the group. (2) FLAG-003: Added new Step 9 to resolve `originTrackId` in `buildPendingDragSession` at `useClipDrag.helpers.ts:285`, preventing the drag abort when `planMultiDragMoves` looks up a track that `handleClearUnusedTracks` has already removed. Also added drag session test files to the validation step.

flags.concern: Shot group status: the plan fixes `useShotGroups` to project groups onto the resolved row, but it does not update `useShotGroupHandlers`, which still records stale-video ids as `${shotId}:${group.trackId}`. `TimelineCanvas` checks stale status against `${shotId}:${group.rowId}`, so stale final-video badges will still disappear for moved groups.
flags.evidence: `useShotGroupHandlers.ts:73-98` builds `staleShotGroupIds` from stored `group.trackId`, while `TimelineCanvas.tsx:270-286` renders `hasStaleVideo` by looking up `${group.shotId}:${group.rowId}` from the projected shot-group overlay data.

flags.concern: Grouped drag: the plan leaves one real stale-track read in the drag path. `buildPendingDragSession()` captures `originTrackId` from the stored group, and `planMultiDragMoves()` aborts if that track id is missing. After clips move off the old row and `handleClearUnusedTracks()` removes the now-empty track, dragging the pinned group can still fail even if the planned read-site fixes land elsewhere.
flags.evidence: Addressed two significant flags: (1) FLAG-001: Step 5 now explicitly separates `storedTrackId` (for identity-matching against `workingPinnedShotGroups` candidates at lines 365, 370-371) from `resolvedTrackId` (for row/data access), and includes the corrected trackId in the nextGroups output. Previously the plan undercounted stale reads in the sync loop and the self-heal could cause the later map/filter to stop matching the group. (2) FLAG-003: Added new Step 9 to resolve `originTrackId` in `buildPendingDragSession` at `useClipDrag.helpers.ts:285`, preventing the drag abort when `planMultiDragMoves` looks up a track that `handleClearUnusedTracks` has already removed. Also added drag session test files to the validation step.
